_meta.compiled_for: EdGame business plan (KAUST capstone, $500K seed @ $4M pre)
_meta.notes: All figures verified against secondary source where possible. Where two sources give different numbers, both are listed with the more authoritative cited first. 'USD' figures are nominal year-of-source unless noted.

market.claim: Global EdTech expenditure is projected to reach $404B by 2025, representing a 16.3% CAGR from 2019-2025 (2.5x growth over the period)
market.source: HolonIQ — Global EdTech Market to Reach $404B by 2025

market.claim: The total global education market (all of education, not just tech) is projected to reach nearly $10T by 2030, with Early Childhood and Workforce as fastest-growing segments
Global EdTech venture capital reached $2.4B in 2025, up from $1.8B in 2024 (the lowest level since 2014); deals are smaller, more disciplined, and skewed toward AI-enabled, workforce-aligned models
market.source: HolonIQ — EdTech funding drops again in early 2025

market.claim: Cumulative global EdTech VC funding through 2030 is projected to surpass $87B (vs. $32B over the entire prior decade)
market.source: HolonIQ — $87bn+ of Global EdTech funding predicted through 2030

market.claim: Global game-based learning market was $13.17B in 2024, projected to grow to $49.52B by 2035 at ~12.79% CAGR (Market Research Future). Other firms give larger figures up to $88-191B by 2035
market.value: $13.17B (2024) -> $49.52B (2035) at 12.79% CAGR (MRF base case); alt estimates $88-191B at 15-22% CAGR
market.source: Market Research Future — Game-Based Learning Market

market.claim: The K-12 segment of global game-based learning is projected to reach $17.0B by 2035 (MRF)
market.source: Market Research Future — Game-Based Learning Market

market.claim: GCC EdTech market was valued at $3.02B in 2024 and is projected to reach $4.47B by 2030 at a 6.74% CAGR (MarkNtel)
Saudi Arabia EdTech market was $2.32B in 2024 and is projected to reach $6.85B by 2033, and Saudi Arabia overtook Egypt as the largest MENA EdTech HQ region in HolonIQ's 2025 rankings
market.source: HolonIQ — 2024 Middle East & North Africa EdTech 50

market.claim: Middle East EdTech market size (broad/regional definition) was $264.2B in 2024 with 16.6% CAGR projected to $573.1B by 2032 (Verified Market Research — broader definition includes adjacent services)
market.source: Verified Market Research — Middle East EdTech Market

competitors.name: Kahoot!
competitors.funding: $584M raised; IPO Oct 2019 on Euronext Oslo; delisted March 19, 2024 after Goldman Sachs/General Atlantic-led take-private
competitors.valuation: £1.18B (~$1.5B) market cap at delisting in March 2024; enterprise value ~$1.66B post-delisting

competitors.name: Prodigy Education (Prodigy Math)
competitors.users: 100M+ registered students globally per company claims
competitors.valuation: Private; no confirmed IXL acquisition found in search results — Prodigy remains independent

competitors.name: IXL Learning
competitors.valuation: Private; aggressive acquirer — bought WyzAnt (Apr 2024), Carson Dellosa (Aug 2024), MyTutor (May 2025), Evan-Moor (Oct 2025), Rosetta Stone (Feb 2026)

competitors.name: Legends of Learning
competitors.funding: $18.6M-$27.3M total across 5 rounds; $5M seed led by Konvoy Ventures with angels including Fitbit CEO James Park, Lightspeed's Jeremy Liew, Crunchyroll's Kun Gao
competitors.users: K-8 math + science games; 2M+ students per company claims
competitors.valuation: Private; small relative to category leaders

competitors.name: Classcraft
competitors.funding: Acquired by Houghton Mifflin Harcourt (HMH) in 2023; relaunched as 'HMH Classcraft' in Feb 2024
competitors.users: Original platform shut down June 30, 2024; rebuilt version excludes individual teachers (district-only); created a major market gap among individual K-12 teachers
competitors.source: HMH press release / Change.org petition / educatoral.com

competitors.name: Minecraft: Education Edition
competitors.funding: Microsoft-owned (acquired Mojang 2014 for $2.5B); no separate education funding rounds
competitors.users: Available in 115+ countries, 35+ languages; used in 100M+ classrooms cumulatively per Microsoft
competitors.valuation: Subsidiary of Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT, ~$3T market cap)

competitors.name: Duolingo
competitors.funding: Pre-IPO ~$183M raised; IPO July 2021 at $6.5B valuation (NASDAQ: DUOL)
competitors.valuation: ~$5B market cap as of May 2026 (down ~79% YoY from 2024-25 peak amid AI-substitution concerns)

competitors.name: Khan Academy
competitors.revenue: $107.3M revenue in 2023 (75% from foundation + individual donors; $64.2M from institutional/major donors; $15.3M corporate; $12M earned)
competitors.valuation: N/A (non-profit). Major funders: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, Beneficus Foundation; in-kind support from Google, Microsoft, Adobe, Walmart

competitors.name: Brainly
competitors.funding: $148.5M raised; largest round was $80M Series D (Dec 2020) led by Learn Capital
competitors.users: 350M+ registered users globally per company claims
competitors.valuation: Private; last priced round (2020) implied ~$500M+ valuation; no public 2024-25 valuation

competitors.name: Quizlet
competitors.funding: $62M total raised across 3 rounds; $30M Series C in May 2020 from General Atlantic + Union Square Ventures + Icon Ventures at $1B valuation (unicorn)
competitors.users: 30M+ MAU; used by 2/3 of US high schoolers and 1/2 of US college students

vision2030.claim: Saudi Arabia's 2025 budget allocates SAR 201B (~$53.5B) to education — 16% of total government expenditures, the single largest line item
vision2030.source: KPMG Saudi Arabia Budget Report 2025 / Asharq Al-Awsat

vision2030.claim: Saudi Arabia spent $52B on education in 2022 + an additional $7B under the National Transformation Program for school modernization through 2030
More than 6 million Saudi students will be reached by the nationwide AI curriculum starting in the 2025-2026 academic year
vision2030.source: Arab News — Saudi Arabia's 2025 education plan

vision2030.claim: Over 400 new private schools opened in Saudi Arabia in 2024, marking the fastest private-school growth phase in recent KSA history

NEOM has announced ~$10B in investment for its education, research, and innovation sector, including NEOM U (online classes launching 2025), the Education Research and Innovation Foundation, Talent Academy, and the Neom Community School (operating since 2021, K-11 as of Feb 2025)
vision2030.source: Arab News / NEOM / Arab Education Magazine

vision2030.claim: KAUST Innovation Ventures invests $200K (seed) up to $2M (early-stage) in deep-tech startups across energy, water, food, ICT, and healthcare; KAUST runs the National Transformation Institute as an innovation catalyst

Saudi Vision 2030's Human Capability Development Program (launched 2021 by Crown Prince MBS) targets sending 70,000 Saudis to top-200 global institutions by 2030; kindergarten enrollment hit 34% in 2023, surpassing the 32% target
vision2030.source: Saudi Vision 2030 — Human Capability Development Program

regulation.claim: COPPA (US): EdTech vendors must obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting personal data from children under 13; the K-12 'school consent exception' allows schools to consent on behalf of parents — this is the legal mechanism enabling B2B-K12 EdTech at scale
regulation.source: AI Governance Group — FERPA, COPPA, and Beyond

regulation.claim: FERPA (US): Federal law protecting student education records; EdTech vendors are treated as 'school officials' and must sign data-processing agreements limiting use, ensuring security, and supporting access/deletion requests

Saudi Arabia's Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) became fully enforceable September 14, 2024; explicitly treats minors' data, health data, genetic data, and credit data as 'special types' requiring elevated protection per the Implementing Regulations issued by SDAIA
regulation.value: PDPL effective 14 Sep 2024; minors = special category
regulation.source: Morgan Lewis — Saudi Arabia PDPL Transition Period Ends Sep 14

regulation.claim: Saudi PDPL: Data of minors is processed under stricter rules per the SDAIA Implementing Regulations; cross-border transfer of personal data is restricted and requires SDAIA approval for sensitive categories
regulation.source: ICLG Data Protection Laws and Regulations 2025-2026 — Saudi Arabia

regulation.claim: GDPR (EU/UK): Children under 16 (or 13-16 by member state) require parental consent for data processing; UK's Age Appropriate Design Code adds 15 standards for any online service likely to be accessed by children
US-state student privacy laws: 121+ state laws now exceed FERPA; the Texas SCOPE Act became effective September 1, 2024 imposing additional duties on platforms accessed by minors
regulation.value: 121+ US state laws; SCOPE Act effective Sep 2024
regulation.source: McDermott — EdTech and Privacy Regulatory Landscape

vc_thesis_reach.claim: Reach Capital closed Fund IV in April 2023 at $215M — its largest fund ever — to invest in seed through Series B in K-12, higher-ed, and workforce/reskilling technology
Reach Capital was founded in 2015 by Jennifer Carolan (ex-NewSchools Venture Fund Seed Fund MD); the firm explicitly seeks to 'reject mercenary founders' and invests on 7-10-year K-12 timelines
Reach Capital has made 256 investments to date; Reach Fund II's $32M into 14 core bets produced multiple unicorns including Newsela, Handshake, and Outschool

Reach Capital 2024-2025 deals include Teachy ($7M Series A, Dec 2024 — AI for LATAM/Asia schools, co-led with Goodwater), Tenor ($5.4M seed, Dec 2024), Stepful ($31.5M), Manifold AI, and Songscription — signal AI + workforce focus
vc_thesis_reach.value: Active 2024-25 deals: Teachy, Tenor, Stepful, Manifold

vc_thesis_reach.claim: Reach Capital portfolio includes notable game/play-adjacent K-12 bets: ClassDojo, Tynker, Nearpod, Newsela, BetterLesson, eSpark, Outschool, Schoolzilla, Zeal — pattern is play/engagement + measurable teacher utility
vc_thesis_reach.source: EdSurge — Reach Capital fund I portfolio

vc_thesis_a16z.claim: Marc Andreessen has publicly argued AI tutors will solve Bloom's two-sigma problem (1-on-1 tutoring producing +2 SD outcomes vs. classroom); a16z thesis: 'every child will have an AI tutor — infinitely patient, knowledgeable, helpful'
vc_thesis_a16z.source: a16z — Why AI Will Save the World

vc_thesis_a16z.claim: a16z published 'Five Predictions for the Future of Learning in the Age of AI' (2024) outlining how AI tutors + human supplementation reshape K-12 and higher-ed; thesis tags education as part of the consumer/learning economy
vc_thesis_a16z.value: a16z 'Future of Learning in AI' framework
vc_thesis_a16z.source: a16z — Five Predictions for the Future of Learning

vc_thesis_a16z.claim: Notable a16z education-adjacent investments referenced in firm content: Outschool (live classes marketplace), Numerade (AI tutor 'Ace' for STEM), Lambda School (ISA-based bootcamp), Replit (browser-based coding/education); Connie Chan (GP, consumer) historically covers learning
vc_thesis_a16z.value: a16z edu touchpoints: Outschool, Numerade, Lambda, Replit

vc_thesis_a16z.claim: a16z manages $44B+ AUM across funds (firm-wide); Connie Chan (GP) covers consumer including learning; Anjney Midha (GP from 2023) leads AI infrastructure but has no public education-specific thesis. a16z does not have a dedicated education fund — investments come from consumer/AI/apps funds

a16z 'New Learning Economy: It's Time To Build in Education' (post-COVID) positions edtech opportunity as supplemental + skill-based + creator-economy adjacent — anti-thesis to seat-license institutional sales

edtech_benchmarks.claim: Median EdTech revenue multiple was 1.6x by Q4 2024 (down from 7.2x peak in Q4 2020); seed-stage EdTech EV/Revenue averages ~11.9x (with very wide dispersion 0.2x-80x)
US SaaS seed-stage 2024-25 typical post-money valuations cluster $14-17M (median); mean seed round size is ~$4M (higher for AI-tagged startups)
Series A and B EV/Revenue multiples remain ~10-10.5x but spread narrows substantially; example: $5M ARR + 35% growth + 102% NRR = ~4.1x = ~$20.5M valuation
Education SaaS has fastest CAC payback in B2B SaaS at ~3.8 months (vs. 8.6 months B2B SaaS median) due to ~$42 CAC from efficient teacher-focused social ads
Healthy LTV:CAC ratio is 3:1 minimum, 5:1+ strong; SMB SaaS averages 2.5:1, Enterprise SaaS ($100K+ ACV) averages 4.5:1; median across all B2B SaaS is 3.2:1
Healthy CAC payback targets by segment: <12 months SMB, <18 months Mid-Market, <24 months Enterprise; early-stage <$1M ARR sees median 2-month payback while $50M+ ARR slows to 20 months

research_efficacy.claim: Clark, Tanner-Smith & Killingsworth (2016) meta-analysis: digital games significantly enhance learning vs. non-game conditions, effect size g=0.33 (95% CI 0.19-0.48) across 57 studies (n=209)
research_efficacy.source: Clark et al., Review of Educational Research, 2016

research_efficacy.claim: Wouters et al. (2013) meta-analysis: serious games beat conventional instruction on learning (d=0.29) and retention (d=0.36); single-session interventions did NOT show significant gains — multi-session is the moderator
research_efficacy.source: Wouters et al., Journal of Educational Psychology (via ResearchGate)

research_efficacy.claim: Valerie Shute (FSU) & colleagues established stealth assessment in Physics Playground: in-game telemetry validly correlates with external physics tests, students improved physics understanding after gameplay, and reported enjoyment — the foundational framework EdGame builds on
research_efficacy.source: Shute — Stealth Assessment (MIT Press, open access)

research_efficacy.claim: Crystal Island (NC State, narrative-centered microbiology learning environment) has produced significant learning gains across content + problem-solving measures, consistent across genders, in 40+ peer-reviewed publications and thousands of students across urban/suburban/rural schools
research_efficacy.source: NC State Intellimedia — Crystal Island Research

research_efficacy.claim: Frontiers in Education (2020) replicated stealth-assessment findings using multichannel data from Crystal Island, supporting individualized game-learning analytics as a robust assessment paradigm
research_efficacy.source: Frontiers in Education — Quantifying Scientific Thinking with Crystal Island

gcc_school_groups.claim: GEMS Education operates 60+ schools across MENA + Asia/Europe/N. America serving ~170,000 students from 176+ countries; revenue $539.1M for 6 months ending Feb 2024 (+15% YoY)
GEMS Education: Brookfield-led consortium invested ~$2B in June 2024 at a reported $4B+ valuation, replacing an outright London IPO route; sets benchmark for GCC private-K12 enterprise value
gcc_school_groups.source: Bloomberg — Brookfield Buys Stake in GEMS Education

gcc_school_groups.claim: Taaleem operates 14 schools in the UAE and has publicly stated plans to expand into Bahrain, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia (DFM-listed since 2022)
SABIS Network operates schools across Saudi Arabia (e.g., Abdulaziz International Schools in Riyadh, Knowledge Private International School in Dammam) as part of its global K-12 operator footprint
gcc_school_groups.value: SABIS operates in KSA (Riyadh, Dammam, more)

gcc_school_groups.claim: Al-Andalus Private Schools (Madares Al-Andalus) was established in 1984 in Jeddah and has expanded across multiple KSA cities; AdvancED-accredited; runs Saudi curriculum with Islamic/national values + academic excellence focus
gcc_school_groups.source: Mdares AI — Al Andalus International Schools

classcraft_gap.claim: Classcraft was acquired by Houghton Mifflin Harcourt (HMH) in 2023; the original platform was shut down on June 30, 2024 and replaced by 'HMH Classcraft' — a district-only product that explicitly excludes individual teachers

HMH publicly acknowledged that 'many of the beloved gamification features would be missing initially' from the rebuilt platform, triggering a Change.org petition and an educator exodus to ClassMana, EdQuest, and other alternatives

Educator-reported impact: Classcraft had been a leading individual-teacher gamification platform before the shutdown; the shutdown left a market gap among K-12 teachers who relied on RPG-style classroom mechanics, validating demand for game-mechanic-driven K-12 tools
classcraft_gap.value: Market gap among individual K-12 teachers
